references: EDB Physics Curriculum and Assessment Guide (Secondary 4-6)
EDB senior secondary Physics Compulsory Part learning objectives
Public Hong Kong S4 Physics syllabus topic examples (heat and gases, force and motion, wave motion, electricity and magnetism, radioactivity and nuclear energy, energy and use of energy)
source.note: No fixed official Hong Kong S4 physics vocabulary list is prescribed; this list is theme-based for senior secondary S4 physics enrichment import. Term-only entries are completed during import.

words.definition: The tendency of an object to resist changes in its state of motion.
words.example: Passengers lurch forward when the bus brakes suddenly due to inertia.
words.example_translation: 由於慣性，當巴士突然煞車時，乘客會向前傾倒。

words.definition: The product of an object's mass and velocity, representing the quantity of motion.
words.example: A heavy truck has more momentum than a small car moving at the same speed.
words.example_translation: 在相同速度下，重型卡車的動量大於小型汽車。

words.definition: A collision where both total momentum and total kinetic energy are conserved.
words.example: Billiard balls undergoing an elastic collision bounce off each other without losing energy.
words.example_translation: 進行彈性碰撞的撞球會互相彈開而不會損失能量。

words.definition: The energy stored in an object due to its position in a gravitational field.
words.example: Water held behind a dam possesses high gravitational potential energy.
words.example_translation: 水壩後方儲存的水具有高重力勢能。

words.definition: The energy transferred when a force moves an object over a distance.
words.example: No work is done on a wall if you push against it but it does not move.
words.example_translation: 如果你推牆但牆沒有移動，則對牆沒有做功。

words.definition: The rate at which work is done or energy is transferred.
words.example: A powerful engine can do the same amount of work in less time than a weak one.
words.example_translation: 強勁的引擎能在較短時間內完成與弱引擎相同的功。

words.definition: Periodic motion where the restoring force is directly proportional to the displacement.
words.example: A pendulum swinging with small angles exhibits simple harmonic motion.
words.example_translation: 以小角度擺動的單擺呈現簡諧運動。

words.definition: The gradual reduction of oscillation amplitude due to energy loss.
words.example: Shock absorbers in cars provide damping to smooth out the ride.
words.example_translation: 汽車的避震器提供阻尼以平滑行車體驗。

words.definition: A phenomenon where an object vibrates with greater amplitude at specific frequencies.
words.example: The singer shattered the glass by hitting the note that matched its natural frequency, causing resonance.
words.example_translation: 歌手通過唱出與玻璃固有頻率匹配的音符導致共振，從而擊碎了玻璃。

words.definition: A wave where particle displacement is parallel to the direction of wave propagation.
words.example: Sound waves traveling through air are longitudinal waves.
words.example_translation: 在空氣中傳播的聲波是縱波。

words.definition: A wave where particle displacement is perpendicular to the direction of wave propagation.
words.example: Light waves and waves on a string are examples of transverse waves.
words.example_translation: 光波和弦上的波是橫波的例子。

words.definition: The bending of light as it passes from one medium to another with a different density.
words.example: A straw appears bent in a glass of water due to refraction.
words.example_translation: 由於折射，水中的吸管看起來是彎曲的。

words.definition: The complete reflection of light back into the medium when it hits the boundary at an angle greater than the critical angle.
words.example: Optical fibers use total internal reflection to transmit data over long distances.
words.example_translation: 光纖利用全內反射來長距離傳輸數據。

words.definition: A lens that is thicker in the middle and brings parallel light rays to a focus.
words.example: A magnifying glass is a converging lens used to produce enlarged images.
words.example_translation: 放大鏡是一種用於產生放大影像的凸透鏡。

words.definition: The force of attraction or repulsion between charged particles.
words.example: Rubbing a balloon on hair creates an electrostatic force that makes hair stand up.
words.example_translation: 在頭髮上摩擦氣球會產生靜電力，使頭髮豎起。

words.definition: The rate of flow of electric charge past a point in a circuit.
words.example: Amperes are the units used to measure electric current.
words.example_translation: 安培是用來測量電流的單位。

words.definition: The work done per unit charge to move charge between two points in a circuit.
words.example: Voltage is another common term for potential difference.
words.example_translation: 電壓是電勢差的另一個常見稱呼。

words.definition: A circuit where components are connected end-to-end, so the same current flows through all of them.
words.example: In a series circuit, if one bulb burns out, all bulbs go off.
words.example_translation: 在串聯電路中，如果一個燈泡燒壞，所有燈泡都會熄滅。

words.definition: A circuit where components are connected across the same two points, allowing multiple paths for current.
words.example: Household wiring uses parallel circuits so appliances can operate independently.
words.example_translation: 家庭配線使用並聯電路，以便電器可以獨立運作。

words.definition: The branch of physics dealing with the relationship between electricity and magnetism.
words.example: Electric motors rely on principles of electromagnetism to convert electrical energy into motion.
words.example_translation: 電動機依賴電磁學原理將電能轉換為機械能。
